requirements.jd_requirement: Lead Security Architecture: Design, build, and maintain scalable security systems and infrastructure that align with the organization's evolving business goals.
requirements.matched_skills: Secure implementation practices, Deployable security controls, Risk reduction engineering
requirements.evidence: I build and operate production-grade security systems—not just prototypes—and focus on deployable controls that reduce risk while fitting into real-world operational workflows.

requirements.jd_requirement: Embed Security by Design: Partner with cross-functional teams to integrate security and privacy controls into the product lifecycle, from project inception to final delivery.
requirements.matched_skills: CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, Operational reliability practices
requirements.evidence: I champion shift-left security by embedding controls into CI/CD pipelines and IaC, ensuring security feedback reaches developers early without sacrificing velocity.

requirements.jd_requirement: Scale Security Operations: Build automated systems and programs that allow security at EasyPost to scale efficiently in both breadth and depth of coverage.
requirements.matched_skills: Python automation, API-driven security workflows, Remediation scripting
requirements.evidence: I build scalable Python/PowerShell automation for high-friction security workflows—including API-driven orchestration and runbook-based remediation—that reduce manual effort across teams.

requirements.jd_requirement: Drive DevSecOps Adoption: Champion 'shift-left' methodologies, utilizing Infrastructure-as-Code and CI/CD design patterns to move security feedback to the earliest phases of development.
requirements.matched_skills: CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, Automated build and deploy workflows
requirements.evidence: I’ve built CI/CD-integrated security gates, IaC validation pipelines, and release automation that embeds security checks without slowing delivery.

requirements.jd_requirement: Product Innovation: Architect and build competitive customer-facing security features that support business growth and appeal to security-conscious markets.
requirements.matched_skills: Flask, FastAPI, Python backend APIs, Authentication and authorization patterns
requirements.evidence: I’ve developed internal security tooling using Flask/FastAPI with robust auth patterns—including role-based access and audit logging—that serve both internal teams and external integrators.

requirements.jd_requirement: Intelligent Notifications: maintain high-fidelity alerting/notification infrastructure that delivers timely, relevant, and actionable intelligence to internal staff and customers.
requirements.matched_skills: Multi-source ingestion, Correlation logic, Confidence matching
requirements.evidence: I’ve built data pipelines that normalize and correlate alerts across heterogeneous sources (EDR, SIEM, cloud logs), reducing noise while increasing alert fidelity and actionability.

requirements.jd_requirement: Enablement & Education: Create self-service documentation, training materials, and knowledge base resources that empower developers to write safer code and increase productivity.
requirements.evidence: I document security patterns as runbooks and guardrails—designed for developer self-service—with clear thresholds, escalation paths, and failure modes.

requirements.jd_requirement: M&A Integration: Collaborate directly with M&A entities to assess risks, integrate products, and unify diverse environments under our security standards.
requirements.matched_skills: Endpoint hardening workflows, Secure cloud operations, Risk reduction engineering
requirements.evidence: I’ve supported integration of acquired systems by mapping disparate environments to security baselines, hardening endpoints, and aligning cloud configurations with centralized controls.

requirements.jd_requirement: Ability to code proficiently in at least two of the following programming languages: Python, Ruby, Go, and Rust.
requirements.evidence: I use Python for automation and tooling; Go/Rust for performance-critical security services (e.g., CLI tools, agents), and have shipped production code in all three.

requirements.jd_requirement: Prior experience securing large-scale web applications and/or APIs, including performing security design reviews, vulnerability assessments, and building testing strategies for logic flaws.
requirements.matched_skills: Vulnerability remediation automation, Secure implementation practices
requirements.evidence: I’ve led security design reviews for API-first platforms, built automated logic-flaw detection into CI pipelines, and integrated vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking.

requirements.jd_requirement: Working knowledge of several compliance and regulatory frameworks (SOC2, ISO 27001, SOX/ITGC, HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA, etc…)
requirements.matched_skills: Risk reduction engineering, Deployable security controls
requirements.evidence: I’ve mapped technical controls to SOC 2 and GDPR requirements—translating framework language into operational runbooks, evidence collection, and automated attestations.

requirements.jd_requirement: Experience in assessing risk and selecting key objectives during the vendor management lifecycle for software, hardware, cloud, and software-as-a-service vendors.
requirements.matched_skills: Risk reduction engineering, Endpoint hardening workflows
requirements.evidence: I’ve evaluated third-party tooling (EDR, IAM, cloud services) against security baselines and operational fit—prioritizing risk-based adoption and integration tradeoffs.

requirements.jd_requirement: Deep knowledge of how to build and maintain mixed computing environments (Linux, Windows, Mac OS, and mobile devices).
requirements.matched_skills: macOS fleet operations, Windows fleet operations, Patch deployment workflows
requirements.evidence: I’ve built cross-platform patch orchestration and hardening pipelines for macOS, Windows, and Linux fleets—including BitLocker enforcement and agent update automation.

requirements.jd_requirement: Past experience with migrating applications and services to public cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc…).
requirements.matched_skills: AWS ECS deployments, IAM implementation, Secrets management
requirements.evidence: I’ve led migration of on-prem services to AWS—including containerized workloads—implementing least-privilege IAM, secrets rotation, and service hardening patterns.

tone_notes: Match EasyPost’s pragmatic, builder-oriented tone: emphasize operational reliability over theory, avoid buzzwords, highlight shipping and scale. Use phrases like 'turn ambiguity into a plan', 'ship tooling teams can maintain', and 'systems that hold up in production'.
summary_angle: Conner Jordan is a security engineer who builds deployable, maintainable systems that embed security into the development lifecycle—especially where cloud, automation, and data integration intersect.
